canonical_s8_baseline.threshold_effect: fails throughput geomean, every-scenario throughput, p50, p95, flagship, and CPU gates

best_retained_runtime_candidate.candidate: stacked FIFO wake suppression plus dequeue driver/wake_yield fast path
best_retained_runtime_candidate.threshold_effect: still fails throughput geomean, every-scenario throughput, p50, p95, and flagship gates

best_substrate_counterexample.candidate: tokio_base dynamic-budget same public client path
best_substrate_counterexample.threshold_effect: runtime substrate can improve CPU but still fails throughput and latency gates

minimum_additional_effect_from_best_reta.interpretation: A remaining candidate must be architecture-scale: another micro-optimization in request formatting, pool checkout, or TLS wrapper polling cannot plausibly close this gap.

completion_gate_coverage.no_surface_case_has_external_next_decisi: not applicable because one S9F surface remains conditionally implementation-plausible

surface_inventory.reason: Protocol, proxy TLS, mTLS, and shared semantics are correctness/security surfaces already covered by S1-S7 evidence. Changing them would risk semantics and cannot explain the remaining nested HTTPS-origin response-read gap.

measured_bounds.pool_checkout_us_per_request: about 3 us in failing concurrent phase diagnostics
measured_bounds.body_drain_us_per_request: about 1.5 us in failing concurrent phase diagnostics
measured_bounds.response_read_us_per_request: about 1.0-1.3 ms in failing HTTPS-origin proxy cells

surface_inventory.reason: Even perfect elimination of pool checkout, request formatting, or body drain is far below the roughly 1.6x-1.9x throughput/tail-latency correction still required after the best retained candidate.

metrics.corrected_tls_shape: HTTPS-origin proxy cells average about 5 SSL_read calls and 2 SSL-read pendings per request, not a call-count explosion

surface_inventory.reason: The dedicated nested TLS stream variant and progress-aware retry were tested and rejected. ylong_base and tokio_base also show identical nested pending counts in the readiness profile, so OpenSSL wrapper pending amplification is not a ylong-specific SOTA lever.

Benchmark-only scheduling rewrites cannot support real-client-path SOTA evidence, and fair public-client model variants still fail throughput/tail gates. Harness work may improve measurement clarity, not the S9F performance outcome.
TLS version/cipher mismatch is refuted by fingerprint diagnostics, OpenSSL release-buffers regresses, and swapping runtime substrate improves CPU but does not meet fixed SOTA thresholds.

Worker count, block_on driving, periodic driver checks, generic park/yield/steal tweaks, and optimistic readiness-before-wait have all been rejected or fall far short. Repeating them would violate A2 forbidden substitutes.

causal_basis.worker_migration: 27/32 requests touched multiple workers and 8/8 measured task ids appeared on multiple workers
causal_basis.correlation: write_done_to_response_ready correlates with park events r=0.9587 and steal/search events r=0.9404
causal_basis.wake_to_poll_latency: wake-to-next-poll p95 is only 145.871 us, so the issue is not post-wake dispatch alone
causal_basis.syscall_shape: ylong has comparable recvfrom/sendto counts to libcurl but much higher futex, clock_nanosleep, epoll_wait, and sched_yield counts under strace
causal_basis.source_observation: ScheduleIO::wake0 calls stored task wakers with wake_by_ref; the task schedule path enqueues based on current runtime context and can place IO-woken work on the driver/current worker rather than a stable task-local worker

minimum_measurable_hypothesis_for_s9f.hypothesis: A worker-affine IO readiness wake path that preserves HTTP/TLS semantics and reduces cross-worker migration/steal/park churn around nested TLS response-read can close a SOTA-scale part of the remaining gap.
minimum_measurable_hypothesis_for_s9f.required_red_diagnostic: Before production change, trace IO readiness wake worker, task last-polled worker, enqueue worker, and next poll worker for HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy concurrency=8; show same-task wake locality mismatch or cross-worker migration in the slow cells.
minimum_measurable_hypothesis_for_s9f.candidate_shape: Attach or derive task-locality information for IO readiness wake scheduling, then prefer enqueueing IO-woken tasks to the last-polled/home worker without changing readiness bits, TLS reads, CONNECT semantics, or public client API.
minimum_measurable_hypothesis_for_s9f.canonical_pass_bar: unchanged fixed SOTA gate from requirements.control.json; no focused run can replace S9F canonical matrix

surface_inventory.risk: This is architecture-level runtime scheduling work. A narrow micro-tweak is unlikely to satisfy the hypothesis; if the red diagnostic does not show locality mismatch, S9F must reject this surface rather than patching.

frontier_decision.s9f_next_action: Start S9F by adding a diagnostic-only red test/trace for IO readiness wake worker locality in ylong_runtime nested HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy. Do not change production scheduling before the diagnostic reproduces the mismatch.
forbidden_followups: Do not mark O-causal-performance-evidence complete from this S9E evidence.
Do not re-run rejected micro-candidates without new contradictory evidence.
Do not use benchmark-only single-driver or runtime-substrate substitution as real-client SOTA evidence.
